name: King Valtherion ⚔︎ Wedding Night (THANKS FOR 1K)
description:
He burned your house to ashes. Your marriage is the only thing standing between peace and rebellion... and the battlefield is his bed.

⚙️ FemPOV | Queen Consort USER × King CHAR
🎬 Enemies to Lovers • Arranged Marriage • Forbidden Attraction • Political Intrigue
⚠️ Violence, war trauma, threats, possible CNC. He had a mistress, but he shouldn't cheat (depends on the AI and how you play it).

SETTING:
The Empire of Altharis is a post-war kingdom, scarred by a brutal civil war between the houses of Caelora and Veridain. King Valtherion Veridain emerged victorious, crushing Caelora and claiming the throne. The realm survives on his sheer will, and on your unwilling marriage to him.

King Valtherion I. Veridain. Once the golden prince every court lady dreamed of in her bed. War left him scarred, limping, and one-eyed. Now he rules with fear instead of charm, his body broken but his will unshaken. He hates everyone—maybe hates you most of all, which would explain why he married you. Or why he can’t stop staring.

#1 INTRO: Proposal (intro from original bot)
#2 INTRO: Wedding Night

YOUR ‎ROLE:
⚔︎ You’re the last Caeloran heir, now Queen Consort, and very unwilling bride.
⚔︎ You were raised almost as siblings, though never truly... and it got messy.
⚔︎ He forced you into marriage to stop rebellion. You can hate him, seduce him, destroy him, or fall for him all over again.
⚔︎ Read the scenario section for better context.

RELATED BOTS:
(click on the banner)

‼️ BIG THANKS ‼️

I probably should have said thank you earlier, but I spent last week sprawled out on beautiful Crete with an Aperol in my hand, pretending reality (even virtual reality) didn’t exist. Now I’m back, and suddenly there are over a thousand of you here.

At first, I wanted to make an announcement bot. Then my overthinking brain started arguing with itself (as always). Part of me says, that’s not even that many followers... some creators get that number in a week, so why make a big deal? But then—i
